title: ED minor head injury documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports mechanism and time, loss of consciousness, headache, nausea or vomiting, dizziness, blurred vision, amnesia or confusion, medication and bleeding-risk review, vital signs, Glasgow Coma Scale, pupils, neurological screen, scalp, and neck documentation.
assessment.rationale: The exact DHA section supports onset, mechanism, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context, vital data, focused examination and appearance only when assessed; it does not classify injury severity automatically.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Minor Head Injury
source.population: Adults and children with minor head injury; red-flag, anticoagulated, non-accidental, or deteriorating presentations require escalation.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth minor-head-injury assessment, warning signs, imaging context, advice, and escalation; concussion diagnosis and local imaging decisions remain clinician-led.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports mechanism and time, loss of consciousness, headache, nausea or vomiting, dizziness, blurred vision, amnesia or confusion, medication and bleeding-risk review, vital signs, Glasgow Coma Scale, pupils, neurological screen, scalp, and neck documentation.

assessment.text: Lists loss of consciousness, seizure, repeated vomiting, drowsiness, worsening headache, amnesia, focal neurological or balance or speech or vision changes, skull or penetrating injury, bleeding disorder or anticoagulant use, intoxication, and non-accidental injury concerns.
assessment.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports recording time-critical symptoms only when clinician-assessed.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Minor Head Injury
source.population: Adults and children with minor head injury; red-flag, anticoagulated, non-accidental, or deteriorating presentations require escalation.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth minor-head-injury assessment, warning signs, imaging context, advice, and escalation; concussion diagnosis and local imaging decisions remain clinician-led.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists loss of consciousness, seizure, repeated vomiting, drowsiness, worsening headache, amnesia, focal neurological or balance or speech or vision changes, skull or penetrating injury, bleeding disorder or anticoagulant use, intoxication, and non-accidental injury concerns.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-determined CT, MRI, or X-ray documentation and immediate referral or escalation when serious-injury criteria are present.
assessment.rationale: The exact investigations and referral section supports recording existing imaging or tests, escalation and specialist discussion only when performed or decided.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Minor Head Injury
source.population: Adults and children with minor head injury; red-flag, anticoagulated, non-accidental, or deteriorating presentations require escalation.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth minor-head-injury assessment, warning signs, imaging context, advice, and escalation; concussion diagnosis and local imaging decisions remain clinician-led.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-determined CT, MRI, or X-ray documentation and immediate referral or escalation when serious-injury criteria are present.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-documented observation, head-injury advice, analgesia decisions, worsening-symptom return precautions, and arranged follow-up without fixed routine timing or doses.
assessment.rationale: The exact observation section supports recording a clinician plan and arranged handover or follow-up only when completed; no duration or instruction is generated.
